signals.title: EUR/USD — ECB Policy Shift + Correlation Break
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1705 — ECB's Kazimir and Kažimír both signal a June rate hike is 'all but inevitable,' shifting the bias from the current 2% hold. Despite this hawkish pivot, EUR/USD vs EuroStoxx correlation has spiked to +0.724 (z=+1.89σ), deviating from the -0.142 norm. This suggests EUR is currently trading as a risk asset rather than on rate differentials. Fed 3.75% vs ECB 2% (+175bp USD advantage) continues to anchor the pair until the June hike is fully priced. Eurozone Manufacturing PMI at 52.2 confirms expansion, but record cost pressures from energy risks limit upside.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Retail Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7183 — COT LF net long +48,299 (74.8% long) indicates heavy institutional backing, yet retail sentiment is 89% short. This extreme divergence suggests retail is aggressively fading the institutional trend. While RBA 4.10% vs Fed 3.75% provides a +35bp carry premium for AUD, the pair has pulled back from 46-month highs as WTI Oil at $106.7 (+4.68%) fuels global inflation/risk-off concerns. Retail capitulation risk is high if price retests 0.7227.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5888 — COT LF net short -16,833 (specs 23% long) shows heavy positioning against the NZD, directly contradicting the RBNZ's hawkish bias and market expectations for a hike at the May 27 meeting. Fed 3.75% vs RBNZ 2.25% currently offers a +150bp USD carry advantage, but a hawkish RBNZ delivery risks a significant short-squeeze of crowded speculative shorts. Watch for a break above 0.5950 to confirm a trend reversal.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Carry vs Geopolitical Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY at 157.08 — Fed 3.75% vs BoJ 0.75% (+300bp carry) remains the primary driver, keeping the pair bid despite JPY net short positioning at -88,543 (crowded carry). VIX at 18.8 (normal volatility) suggests carry remains the dominant regime, but US-Iran conflict headlines and Strait of Hormuz risks introduce intervention potential. 160.00 remains the critical watch level for BoJ verbal warnings; carry dominance persists unless VIX exceeds 25.
